edit dosboxTR.conf, change line glide=true to glide=emu
title: Tomb Raider I

notes: edit dosboxTR.conf, change line glide=true to glide=emu fixes the game but still pretty poor performance
title: Tomb Raider I

notes: Using glide=emu in dosboxTR.conf. It performs at 20-30 fps, but that's quite accurate for this game and I can't compare it to windows, so reporting as silver for now.
title: Tomb Raider I

gpu: X.Org AMD Radeon (TM) RX 480 Graphics (POLARIS10, DRM 3.19.0, 4.14.75-1-lts, LLVM 7.0.0)
notes: The method I used to get the game working is first I went to the game's directory and edited dosboxtr.conf, I change output=direct3d to output=opengl, then I installed a glide wrapper called OpenGlide. (You can find it on the AUR if you're on an arch-based distro), the cutscenes wouldn't play, but the game itself was perfectly playable.
title: Tomb Raider I

notes:
"The method I used to get the game working is first I went to the game's directory and edited dosboxtr.conf, I change output=direct3d to output=opengl, then I installed a glide wrapper called OpenGlide. (You can find it on the AUR if you're on an arch-based distro), the cutscenes wouldn't play, but the game itself was perfectly playable."

- I tried this method but didn't work for me (maybe I did something wrong).
When I started the game with this metod the game did something between 0 and 1 fps and I had to reboot because I also coudn't exit the game.
title: Tomb Raider I

notes:
The main game and the tutorial run fine. Both have been tested several hours. Performance is smooth, and the 3dfx Glide plugin works nicely.

The only thing that doesn't work is massive tearing during cut-scenes (the video ones, not the in-game ones). Video is still distinguishable, but barely. Changing renderer from opengl to another value doesnt help.

Also, alt-tabbing to desktop crashes the game.
